# Feature: Run Reporting — Flow Run HTML Report

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-004
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_run_reporting.md`

Add an "Export Report" button to [FlowResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowResultsPanel.tsx) that, on click, builds a single-file HTML report of the latest flow run and downloads it via the `exportFile` adapter (FEAT-001). The flow report mirrors the visual structure of the panel itself: a flat ordered list of node results, each rendered through the shared `renderRequestDetail` block.

This feature explicitly does **not** render the flow graph (nodes + connectors). The plan deferred graph visualization to keep v1 small. Node ordering matches the order produced by `flow.nodes.map(...)` in `FlowResultsPanel`.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature adds one new builder input type and extends the existing `useReportExport` hook with a flow-export method.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`FlowReportInput`** (new type in `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/flowRun.ts`)
  - `metadata` (`ReportMetadata`) — populated with `runType: 'flow'`, the flow's `name` for `subjectName`, `environmentName` and `defaultAuthName` from whatever the flow runner resolved at run time (or `undefined` if the flow doesn't expose them), `concurrentCalls` and `delayBetweenCalls` are `undefined` for flows, `startedAt` / `completedAt` from `FlowRunResult.startedAt` / `completedAt`, `totalElapsedMs` derived.
  - `summary` (`ReportSummary`) — derived from `FlowRunResult.progress` (map `succeeded → passed`).
  - `nodes` (`ReportRequestNode[]`) — built by walking `flow.nodes` in order, looking up the request via `findRequestMeta(node.requestId, collections)` (the same helper [FlowResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowResultsPanel.tsx) uses) and merging in the matching `FlowNodeResult` from `result.nodeResults.get(node.id)`.

- **`useReportExport` extension** (extends FEAT-003)
  - New method `exportFlowRun(input: FlowReportInput): Promise<void>` with the same status-machine semantics as `exportCollectionRun`.

### Relationships

- N/A.

### Indexes & Constraints

- The Export button is disabled when (a) `result === null`, (b) `result.status === 'running'`, or (c) `reportStatus.state === 'generating'`.

---

- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/flowRun.ts` — `buildFlowRunReport(input: FlowReportInput): string`.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/index.ts` — Re-export the new builder + input type.

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** — `IStorageAdapter.exportFile`.
- **FEAT-002** — Foundation module (shell, header, summary, request detail, format helpers).
- **FEAT-003** — `useReportExport` hook plus the existing `exportCollectionRun` plumbing (this feature *extends* the hook rather than introducing it from scratch).

---

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Implement `buildFlowRunReport`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/flowRun.ts`, implement `buildFlowRunReport(input)` composing `renderShell({ title: \`Wave Flow Run — ${metadata.subjectName}\`, body })` where `body = renderHeader(metadata) + renderSummary(summary) + <section class="items">...</section>`. The items section is `input.nodes.map(renderRequestDetail).join('')` — same shared block as the collection report. Order matches `flow.nodes` order from the input. The collection-resolution / metadata-building logic stays in the panel (TASK-003) — the builder accepts already-resolved `ReportRequestNode` items.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `flowRun.test.ts` covers (1) happy path: 4 nodes with mixed statuses (success / failed / skipped / pending) → 4 cards rendered, summary tiles match. (2) Empty `nodes` array → valid shell with summary zeros. (3) XSS regression: `<script>` injected into the flow name (`subjectName`) and a node `name` is escaped. (4) Order is preserved from the input array. (5) Skipped nodes show the "Skipped" indicator and the existing `error: 'Skipped (condition not met)'` message in the response panel.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the builder + input type. Update `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/index.ts` to re-export.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/builders/flowRun` passes.

### TASK-002: Extend `useReportExport` with `exportFlowRun`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/hooks/useReportExport.ts`, add `exportFlowRun(input: FlowReportInput): Promise<void>` mirroring `exportCollectionRun`: state transitions `idle → generating → success | error`, calls `buildFlowRunReport(input)`, then `adapter.storage.exportFile(buildReportFilename('flow', metadata.subjectName, new Date()), html, 'text/html')`. Same error-surface contract: `notification.showNotification('error', ...)` on `Result.isErr`, defense-in-depth `try/catch` around the builder call.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Extend `useReportExport.test.tsx` to cover the flow path: (1) successful flow export with the right filename prefix (`wave-flow-...`) and the right `mimeType` (`text/html`). (2) Adapter error surfaces via notification. (3) Hook state machine transitions are independent for collection vs flow exports — calling `exportFlowRun` does not interfere with a prior `exportCollectionRun` final state.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new method.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- useReportExport` passes.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

### TASK-003: Wire Export button into `FlowResultsPanel`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In [FlowResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowResultsPanel.tsx), add an Export button to the existing header row, beside the existing `Trash2` Clear Results button. Use Lucide `DownloadIcon` inside a `Button variant="ghost" size="sm"`. Disabled when `result === null || result.status === 'running' || reportStatus.state === 'generating'`. Tooltip: "Export HTML report". On click, build a `FlowReportInput`: (a) `metadata` from the `flow` and `result` fields (`subjectName: flow.name`, `startedAt: result.startedAt`, `completedAt: result.completedAt`, `totalElapsedMs: completedAt - startedAt`, `environmentName` / `defaultAuthName: undefined` for now — the flow runner does not currently surface them; tracked in TODO.md). (b) `summary` derived from `result.progress` (map `succeeded → passed`). (c) `nodes` built by walking `flow.nodes` and reusing the same `findRequestMeta` + `buildRunRequestData` helpers already present in the panel — extract `buildRunRequestData` to a shared location if needed (within the same panel file is acceptable; keep the change minimal). Pass the input to `useReportExport().exportFlowRun(input)`. While generating, swap the download icon for a spinning `Loader2`. On success, show a transient check icon for ~3 s.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `FlowResultsPanel.test.tsx`: (1) Export button renders. (2) Disabled when `result === null`. (3) Disabled while `result.status === 'running'`. (4) Click invokes `exportFlowRun` once with input whose `nodes` length equals `flow.nodes.length`. (5) On error, the error notification surface is called.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the panel's new export-related local state. Inline comment near the metadata-building block noting that `environmentName` / `defaultAuthName` are intentionally `undefined` until the flow runner surfaces them (and link to the TODO.md item).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- FlowResultsPanel` passes. Manual smoke: run a small flow in dev, click Export, confirm download + interactive HTML.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: Flow runner does not currently expose the resolved default auth name to the panel. The metadata field is `undefined` for now, which the foundation header block already handles by omitting the row. A TODO is tracked to surface it later (`TODO.md` → "Per-request resolved auth recorded by runners and surfaced in the report header").
- **Error Handling**: Reuses the FEAT-003 hook contract — `Result<T, E>` from the adapter, `notification.showNotification('error', ...)` on failure, defense-in-depth `try/catch` around the builder.
- **Logging**: N/A.
